questions.question: Explain why using 'Bridged' networking for a malware analysis VM is considered a dangerous practice. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Bridged networking connects the VM directly to the host's physical network.
This gives the malware potential access to the local network (LAN) and the internet.
It could allow the malware to infect other devices, call its C2 server, or exfiltrate data.

questions.question: Describe the step-by-step procedure for safely executing a new malware sample in a virtualized environment, from preparation to cleanup. (8 marks)
expectedPoints: Pre-Analysis: Verify sample hash; transfer sample via secure method (e.g., scanned USB); ensure host AV is updated; log into host as standard user.
VM Setup: Revert analysis VM to a known clean snapshot; confirm Host-Only networking is active; disable shared folders/clipboard sharing.
Execution: Start monitoring tools (Process Monitor, Wireshark); execute the malware; observe behavior.
Post-Analysis: Terminate malware processes; save all logs and artifacts; revert the VM to the clean snapshot again; securely store/encrypt analysis reports and the sample.
